title: Erdős #1052 companion infrastructure: σ* on prime powers and primes
informal_statement: Erdos #1052 (open): are there finitely many unitary perfect numbers? This packet proves only bounded companion infrastructure toward the known (Subbarao-Warren 1966) result that unitary perfect numbers are even -- NOT the open problem itself. Defines uDiv n (unitary divisors of n) and sigmaStar n (the sum of unitary divisors), and proves sigmaStar (p^e) = p^e + 1 for prime p and e >= 1, specializing to sigmaStar p = p + 1 for e = 1. Does NOT include the general multiplicativity theorem sigmaStar_mul_of_coprime (sigmaStar(m*n) = sigmaStar(m)*sigmaStar(n) for coprime m, n) or the structural bound omega_odd_le_two_adic_add_one that depends on it -- both deferred to a future cycle (see notes), and NOT the full even_of_isUnitaryPerfect headline theorem (which additionally needs a Finset.sum_bij' bijection and a Finset.sum_involution argument, also deferred, tracked in this file's companion packets erdos_1052_proper_eq_erase.v1 / erdos_1052_not_dvd_div_ordproj.v1 / erdos_1052_filter_ordcompl.v1).
informal_proof_idea: uDiv n is n's divisors filtered by coprimality of d and n/d; mem_uDiv is a direct unfolding via simp. sigmaStar n sums over uDiv n. sigmaStar_prime_pow shows uDiv (p^e) = {1, p^e} by case-splitting on a divisor's exponent k (0 gives d=1; 0<k<e leads to a contradiction since p would then divide gcd(p^k, p^e/p^k) = 1; k=e gives d=p^e), then sums the two-element set. sigmaStar_prime specializes to e=1.
formal_statement_pp: theorem sigmaStar_prime : ∀ {p : ℕ}, p.Prime → sigmaStar p = p + 1

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; statement cross-checked against the sibling repo's already-kernel_verified proof of the same result

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only bounded companion infrastructure (sigma-star's value on prime powers and primes), not Erdos #1052 itself (genuinely OPEN), and not the general sigmaStar_mul_of_coprime multiplicativity theorem or the omega_odd_le_two_adic_add_one structural bound that depends on it -- both present, already kernel_verified, in the sibling repo's source file, but deferred here since sigmaStar_mul_of_coprime's proof (a 5-branch Finset.sum_nbij' with nested `.`-bullet case splits) is a substantially bigger bullet-free rewrite than this cycle's scope; a future cycle should attempt it, recording any blocker in packets/frontier/erdos/BLOCKERS.md rather than re-discovering this scoping decision from scratch. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 0eb1d5e8, problem_version ae154ca2-2f0a-4000-9324-bf3ef48af1e4), submitted as a SubmitModule (2 defs uDiv/sigmaStar + 2 helper theorems mem_uDiv/sigmaStar_prime_pow + root theorem sigmaStar_prime), kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt. sigmaStar_prime_pow's original sibling-repo proof used nested `.`-bullet case splits (constructor; two bulleted branches, one further split by rcases); rewritten entirely bullet-free (bare sequential tactics per branch, since Lean processes unbulleted goals left-to-right and each branch's tactics fully close their own goal before the next begins) plus proactive parenthesization of every `have h := by tac1; tac2` followed by sibling tactics (issue #67's flattened-sequence trap) -- no other mathematical content changed from the sibling source. Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-1052\proof\Erdos1052_sigmaStar_and_bounds.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review (same policy as this lane's other packets).
